data.description:
## Why

plan-review's adaptive batch orchestration â€” size-ladder escalation, centrality-scaled
budget cap, criteria shedding, and chunk-atomic checkpoint resume â€” lives in the bespoke
`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sizing.py` (312 LOC), OFF the workflow engine. Epic A
(`sage-votary-logic`) added a `BatchRunner` seam + a reference `DefaultBatchRunner` to the
engine. To run plan-review's Pass-1 as an engine `batch` step, the PRODUCTION batching must
plug into that seam without re-implementing the (already-correct, already-tested) sizing logic.

## What

Implement a production `BatchRunner` (the `rebar.llm.workflow.runners.BatchRunner` interface
from epic A) that adapts `sizing.py`'s public surface â€” `pass1_with_ladder()`,
`shed_to_budget()`, `plan_budget_cap()`, `MODEL_LADDER`, and the checkpoint load/save â€” to the
seam's `BatchRunRequest` â†’ `BatchRunResult` contract. It must JOURNAL its batch-plan + fallback
as an OPAQUE plan the interpreter stores but never branches on (the epic-A invariant), and read
the recorded plan on replay. `sizing.py` stays the logic home; this story is the adapter +
wiring, not a rewrite.

## Scope

- New module (e.g. `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/production_batch_runner.py`) implementing the
  `BatchRunner` seam over `sizing.py`.
- Unit tests proving behavioural equivalence with the existing sizing path: batch packing,
  size-ladder escalation (batch â†’ 1-crit â†’ model-ladder â†’ too-big), shed-to-budget order, and
  checkpoint resume by material fingerprint â€” plus seam-conformance (opaque-plan journaling, the
  interpreter-never-branches contract).
- No change to the bespoke orchestrator's behaviour yet (cutover is B5); this only makes the
  production runner available behind the seam.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A production `BatchRunner` implementation adapts `sizing.py` (ladder/budget/shed/checkpoint)
      to epic A's `BatchRunRequest`/`BatchRunResult` seam, journaling an opaque batch-plan.
- [ ] Unit tests assert behavioural equivalence with `sizing.py` for packing, size-ladder
      escalation, shed-to-budget ordering, and checkpoint resume; plus the opaque-plan/no-branch
      seam contract.
- [ ] `sizing.py` remains the single home of the sizing logic (no duplicated algorithm); the new
      module is a thin adapter.
- [ ] `make lint` + the full test suite pass.
data.title: B1: Production batch-runner â€” extract sizing.py behind epic A's BatchRunner seam
